📊 Team Utilization & Performance
Management view only · individual contribution toward project · customer cannot see this
🔒 MANAGEMENT ONLY
Team Efficiency
—
tasks on time / total
At-Risk Members
0
overloaded or stalled
Blocked Tasks
0
waiting on other parties
Project Impact Score
—
team perf → project health
How this works: Personal tasks don't affect project KPIs. Assigned project tasks do. Utilization = (completed assigned tasks on time) / (total assigned tasks). High utilization + high completion = good project velocity. Low utilization may indicate under-assignment. Overloaded members risk delays.
Assign Task
✕
This task will be linked to the project and visible in team utilization reporting. It will appear on the assignee's dashboard.
🗒️ Create Personal Task
✕
Personal tasks are private to you and do not affect project KPIs. Use them for reminders, personal to-dos, and learning items.

🔗 Billing Chain & Contract Structure
Who bills whom · approval flow · PR/PO visibility for all parties
🚚 Material & Equipment Procurement
Track deliveries · flag delays · protect your schedule and billing
Why this matters: If an OEM is on site and the EC hasn't finished terminations, that's an EC delay — the OEM is burning mobilization cost and not billing. Document it here immediately. Same for material deliveries — if conduit doesn't arrive on time, that affects the EC schedule which affects GC milestone which affects energization. Everyone needs to know.
📦 Procurement Log
ItemResponsible PartyNeeded ByExpectedStatusSchedule Impact

✕
📋 Purchase Request
✕
⚠ Log Delay / Standby Event
✕
Document the delay NOW. Who caused it, how long your team is on standby, what it's costing per day, and what it's blocking. This protects your contract and supports any claims.

💬 Ask AI — Role-Aware
🏆 Bonus & Compensation Configuration
Define bonus tiers by performance grade · milestone bonuses · customer satisfaction · admin-only
🔒 ADMIN ONLY
How bonus calculation works: Composite performance grade (A+/A/B/C/D) × Role Base Bonus × Project Milestone Multiplier × Customer Satisfaction Score. This is a framework — actual payouts are at management discretion. The system generates a recommended bonus range per person based on these factors.
📊 Grade-Based Bonus Tiers
💼 Role Category Base Bonus
Starting point before grade multiplier
🎯 Project Milestone Multipliers
⭐ Customer Satisfaction Score
👥 Team Bonus Preview
Recommended ranges — final amounts at management discretion
NameRoleGradeBaseMilestone ×Sat ×Recommended Range
⚙ Performance Metrics Configuration
Define utilization targets, grade thresholds, and metric weights per role — organization-specific standards
This is your organization's performance standard. Every metric, every target, every grade threshold here reflects what YOUR company expects from each role. These settings override the platform defaults and apply to all utilization calculations, team reporting, and performance grades across all projects. Changes take effect immediately.
🧳 Work Mode Adjustments
Shift utilization targets up/down based on how a person works

L5 · IST
L6 · Closeout
🔴 L1 — Factory Witness Testing (FWT)
Red Tag

Verification at the manufacturer's factory before shipment. Only chance to reject or remediate equipment without on-site cost. Managed by CxA + Owner.

Gate to Exit L1
  • ✓ FAT scripts approved by Owner/CxA
  • ✓ All FAT deficiencies resolved & documented
  • ✓ Confirmation-to-Ship certificate issued
  • ✓ O&M manuals and submittals received
  • ✓ Spare parts list confirmed
OEM/Mfg
→
Vendor
→
CxA (witness)
Typical L1 Tests by Asset
Generator
Load test 100% kW · Governor response · Voltage regulation · Paralleling test · Fault simulation · FAT sign-off
UPS
Battery capacity · Transfer time · Overload · Bypass · SNMP/monitoring comm verification
MV Switchgear
Dielectric withstand · Relay calibration · Bus inspection · Control logic simulation · Protective relay settings
Transformer
TTR · Insulation resistance · Power factor · Load/no-load loss · Impedance verification
CRAC/Cooling
Capacity at rated conditions · Refrigerant charge · Controls sim · Alarm points
🟡 L2 — Receipt, Installation & Pre-Energization
Yellow Tag

On-site after delivery. Confirms undamaged receipt, correct installation, pre-energization testing complete. Current active phase on this project.

2.01 BOL/Receipt: shipping docs, damage, model/serial, storage
2.02 Receiving Inspection: detailed vs. submittals, photos, tag
2.03 Installation Inspection (SIP): structural, alignment, clearances, anchoring
2.05a NETA/EC QC: Megger, ductor, torque, grounding
2.05b Vendor Pre-startup verification
2.05c BMS/EPMS: controls wiring, point list, alarm mapping
2.05d MC QC: pressure test, leak test, torque, supports
2.06 Doc Review / Clean Walk: red-lines, as-builts
2.07 Pre-Energization Inspection (PEI)
Gate to Exit L2
  • → All 2.01–2.07 checklists complete + signed
  • → Zero open Critical/High issues
  • → Megger values within NETA limits
  • → Torque complete + Torque-Seal applied
  • → Grounding confirmed ≤1Ω
  • → Pre-energization permit issued
L2 Trade Sequence
Electrical Equipment
GC Receives
→
EC Installs
→
EC/NETA Tests
→
Vendor PFT
→
CxA Signs
Mechanical Equipment
GC Receives
→
MC Installs
→
MC Tests
→
Vendor Startup
→
CxA Signs
Generator / Fuel System
GC Receives
→
EC Power
→
MC Exhaust/Fuel
→
OEM Startup
→
CxA Signs
OEM Liability: Any equipment defect found must be photographed and reported to OEM in writing within 24 hrs before any trade touches it. Issue an NCR immediately.
🟢 L3 — Pre-Functional Testing (PFT)
Green Tag

Individual system startup and standalone testing. Arc flash study must be complete. OEM startup required for all major equipment before NETA acceptance testing begins.

3.01 Startup Prep: arc flash labels, PPE, drawings, energization plan
3.02 First Energization: phasing, voltage, frequency, direction of rotation
3.03 NETA Acceptance Tests: full NETA ATS, signed by Level III tech
3.04 Protective Relay Settings: per coordination study, primary injection
3.05 Vendor Verification: OEM startup complete, report issued, warranty started
3.06 Vendor Pre-Functional Check: control points, alarms, remote monitoring
Gate to Exit L3
  • ✓ NETA acceptance reports signed (Level III)
  • ✓ OEM startup reports received for all assets
  • ✓ All protective relay settings verified
  • ✓ Generator load bank test complete
  • ✓ EPMS/BMS alarms all mapped + verified
L3 Tests: Electrical vs Mechanical
⚡ Electrical L3 Tests
  • ▶ Primary injection (breaker trip verification)
  • ▶ Secondary injection (relay calibration)
  • ▶ Load bank — generator step load 25/50/75/100%
  • ▶ UPS bypass and retransfer test
  • ▶ ATS transfer/retransfer timing
  • ▶ Battery discharge test (UPS autonomy)
  • ▶ PDU branch breaker trip test
🌡️ Mechanical L3 Tests
  • ▶ CRAC/CRAH airflow (CFM) — TAB
  • ▶ Refrigerant leak test at operating pressure
  • ▶ Cooling capacity verification
  • ▶ Chilled water flow / delta-T
  • ▶ Exhaust fan failover test
  • ▶ Fuel system + transfer pump test
  • ▶ Generator exhaust backpressure at load
🔵 L4 — Functional Performance Testing (FPT)
Blue Tag

Systems grouped and tested within discipline. Generators + paralleling switchgear as a set. CRAC + Chiller + BMS as cooling system. Failure scenarios and redundancy validation. Managed by Owner's CxA.

Power: Utility loss → Generators start → ATS transfer → Parallel → Retransfer → Cool-down
UPS: Source loss → Battery ride-through → Generator pickup → Recharge → Bypass
Cooling: Single CRAC failure → Redundant auto-starts → Chiller failover → Economizer
Controls: All EPMS/BMS alarms verified under real fault conditions
StepLoad %DurationAccept
125%30 minStable V/Hz
250%30 minNo alarms
375%30 minOil pressure OK
4100%60 minAll within spec
5110% optional30 minNo overload trip
L4 Sign-Off Requirements
EC / NETA Tech
All L3 electrical tests complete
Required
Mechanical Contractor
All L3 mechanical tests complete
Required
Vendor / OEM
All startup reports submitted
Required
NETA Technician
Acceptance test reports signed
Required
CxA
Level 4 completion report signed
Required
Owner / Client
L4 formal acceptance
Pending
⚪ L5 — Integrated Systems Testing (IST)
White Tag

The culmination. Every system interacts. Full facility load simulation. Cascade failure scenarios. Uptime Institute Tier certification testing occurs here.

Utility Loss — Full Facility
All generators start → parallel → ATSs transfer → IT load live → utility restore → retransfer
N+1 Generator Failure During IST
Utility loss + one gen fails → remaining pick up full load → no IT interruption
Cooling N+1 Failure
CRAC fails → redundant auto-starts → data hall stays within spec
UPS Maintenance Bypass
Transfer to static bypass → maintenance bypass → generator isolation → retransfer
Fire Suppression Trigger
Smoke detected → suppression → power strip → HVAC shutdown → alarms all systems
IST Prerequisites
  • ✕ All L1–L4 punch-list items closed
  • ✕ All NETA acceptance test reports signed
  • ✕ All OEM startup reports received
  • ✕ IST scripts approved by Owner
  • ✕ Load bank equipment on site
  • ✕ Fuel tanks at 100%
  • ✕ All EPMS/BMS alarms mapped
  • ✕ Safety plan approved
IST Deliverables
  • 📄 IST report with all scenario results
  • 📄 Punch list resolution log
  • 📄 DCIM data download + analysis
  • 📄 Uptime Tier certification package
  • 📄 Final O&M manual package
  • 📄 Lessons learned log
L6 — Closeout & Turnover
Turnover Package
  • → Final commissioning report (all levels)
  • → All test reports (NETA, load bank, megger)
  • → O&M manuals all equipment
  • → As-built drawings (red-lines incorporated)
  • → Spare parts inventory
  • → Warranty documentation
  • → Arc flash study + labels
  • → Sequence of Operations (SOO)
  • → Training records
Ongoing Obligations

Uptime Institute Tier certified facilities require quarterly ATS tests and annual full generator load tests. Owner's team must document all ongoing functional tests to maintain certification. CoreSynaptics can provide ongoing commissioning authority services for continuous commissioning post-occupancy.

Asset Manager
All assets — checklist status, phase, readiness, sign-off chain
Asset IDTypeLocPhaseClosedOpenIssuesNext StepReady L3
No assets match filter
Trade Workflow System
Sequential sign-off — each trade releases to the next, no gaps, no liability disputes
How it works: Each trade signs off before the next begins. The asset is locked until prior sign-off is complete. OEM is notified immediately for any suspected manufacturing defect before any trade repairs.
⚡ Electrical Equipment — Sequential Order
1
GC — Receipt & Storage
BOL · Damage photo · Tag issued · Storage plan
2
EC — Installation
Set equipment · Conduit · Cable pull · Termination · Mechanical torque
3
EC/NETA — Pre-Energization
Megger · Hi-Pot · Ductor · Torque · Grounding · NETA report
4
OEM / Vendor — Startup
Factory tech · Energization sequence · Controls · Report issued
5
EC/NETA — Post-Energization
Primary injection · Relay cal · Voltage verify · Phase rotation
6
CxA — Verify & Sign-Off
Witness testing · Review reports · Issue L3 tag · Update tracker
OEM Escalation: Defect found at steps 2–4 → stop work → tag equipment → notify OEM in writing within 24 hrs → issue NCR → await OEM disposition before any repair.
🌡️ Mechanical Equipment — Sequential Order
1
GC — Receipt & Rigging
BOL · Damage · Rigging plan · Storage
2
MC — Mechanical Install
Anchor bolts · Vibration isolators · Piping/refrigerant · Ductwork
3
EC — Electrical Connection
Power termination · Control wiring · Motor megger · Phase rotation
4
MC — Pressure & Leak Test
1.5× MAWP · 10 min hold · Leak sweep · Document
5
OEM — Startup
Refrigerant charge · Controls cal · Sensor verify · Startup report
6
TAB Contractor
CFM balancing · Supply/return delta-T · TAB report
7
CxA — Sign-Off
Witness TAB · Review all docs · Issue L3 green tag
Test Requirements Matrix
Every test × every asset type — NETA ATS, IEEE, ASHRAE, manufacturer standards

Testing Standards Library
What standard · What equipment · How long · Accept criteria
⚡ Electrical Tests
MEGGER / Insulation Resistance
Standard: NETA ATS-2021, IEEE 43-2013 · Voltage: 1kV (LV), 2.5kV (MV), 5kV (HV) DC · Duration: 1 min · Equipment: Megger S1-5010, Fluke 1550C · Accept: >100 MΩ (LV), >1000 MΩ (MV), PI >2.0 · Time: 2–4 hrs per lineup
HI-POT / Dielectric Withstand
Standard: NETA ATS, IEEE C37.09, IEEE 400 · Voltage: 2× rated + 1kV (AC) · Duration: 1 min AC / 5 min DC · Equipment: Phenix Technologies, Megger HVDAC · Accept: No breakdown · Time: 3–6 hrs per bus section
VLF TESTING
Standard: IEEE 400.2, NETA ATS · Frequency: 0.1 Hz sinusoidal · Voltage: 2–3× rated · Duration: 30–60 min soak · Equipment: Baur PHG TD, Megger VLF65 · Used for: MV cable acceptance (preferred over DC for XLPE) · Time: 4–8 hrs per segment
DUCTOR / Contact Resistance
Standard: NETA ATS Table 100.12 · Method: 100A DC 4-wire Kelvin · Equipment: Megger DLRO10, Vanguard EZCT · Accept: Within 50% of similar connections · Time: 1–3 hrs per panel
TRANSFORMER TURNS RATIO (TTR)
Standard: IEEE C57.12.90, NETA ATS · Accept: Within 0.5% of calculated ratio all taps · Equipment: Vanguard ATRT-03A, Megger TTR100 · Time: 1–2 hrs per transformer
LOAD BANK TESTING
Standard: NFPA 110, IEEE 446 · Equipment: Aggreko/Shorepower load banks · Protocol: 25/50/75/100% step load 30 min each · 110% 30 min if specified · Record: Voltage, Hz, temp, oil pressure, fuel consumption · Time: Full day
🔧 Mechanical Tests
PRESSURE TESTING
Standard: ASME B31.3, B31.1, ASHRAE 15 · Pressure: 1.5× MAWP hydro / 1.1× MAWP pneumatic · Duration: Min 10 min hold · Equipment: Hydro test pump, calibrated gauge · Accept: No drop, no leaks · Time: 2–4 hrs per loop
REFRIGERANT LEAK TEST
Standard: ASHRAE 15-2019, EPA Section 608 · Method: Electronic detector + soap bubble + UV dye · Equipment: Fieldpiece SMAN460, Testo 316-3 · Accept: Zero detectable leak · Time: 1–2 hrs per unit
TRANSFORMER TILT TEST
Standard: IEEE C57.12.00, Mfg spec · Purpose: Liquid-filled xfmrs shipped tilted may have oil displacement + gas pockets → insulation damage · Method: Verify plumb/level; check oil dipstick; may require 30-day settling · Time: 1 hr + up to 30 days
TORQUE REPORT
Standard: NETA ATS Table 100.12, ASME B18.2.1 · Method: Calibrated torque wrench + Torque-Seal paint · Equipment: CDI calibrated wrench, Torque-Seal indicator · Record: Bolt size, grade, value, date, tech · Time: 2–6 hrs per asset
EXHAUST BACKPRESSURE
Standard: Generator mfg spec (EGSA, EPA Tier 4) · Equipment: Magnahelic gauge, manometer · Limit: ≤6" W.C. at rated load · Purpose: Excess backpressure reduces output + may void warranty · Time: 1–2 hrs during load test
TAB — AIRFLOW BALANCE
Standard: ASHRAE 111, SMACNA, AABC, NEBB · Equipment: TSI Alnor EBT721, capture hood, anemometer · Accept: ±10% of design CFM per ASHRAE 111 · Time: 2–4 hrs per CRAC; full hall = days

OEM & Vendor Management
Startup visits, defect escalations, NCRs, warranty tracking
CoreSynaptics OEM Protocol: Any suspected manufacturing defect must have an NCR filed within 24 hrs of discovery. No trade may repair or modify equipment pending OEM written disposition. Unauthorized repairs void warranty and create liability.

NCR / Defect Log
No NCRs filed yet
File an NCR when a suspected manufacturing defect is found
NCR must include: Asset ID · Serial # · Date found · Defect description · Photos · Trade who found it · CxA witness · OEM notified within 24 hrs
Lessons Learned
CoreSynaptics field knowledge base — DFW39 project
ProcessL2 · DFW39
Excel formula columns must have cached values before distribution
Active Flag, L2/L3 Scope, Task Include in Checklist Tracker were formula-only with no cached values. LibreOffice and openpyxl show blanks. Fix: Always distribute with computed values in helper columns. Force-calculate before distributing.
ElectricalL2 · Iconix Issues
64 of 75 issues from single subcontractor — pattern indicates process failure
All issues concentrated on Iconix (SIP deficiencies on ACCU/CRAC). Not random — systematic. Action: Formal quality stand-down meeting. Issue NCR if pattern continues. Hold on new work assignments pending closeout.
OEMBest Practice
Schedule OEM startup visits 4–6 weeks in advance
Schneider, Vertiv, Caterpillar, Cummins field service books out 4–6 weeks. Pre-schedule at L1 closeout. Get dates before equipment ships or you wait after L2 is complete.
SafetyBest Practice
Arc flash study must be complete before ANY energized NETA testing at L3
NFPA 70E requires arc flash study + labels before energized testing. Gate arc flash study as L2 deliverable prerequisite. It is a code violation and safety hazard to begin L3 testing without it.
